message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_corrcoef composes through diagonal+sqrt+outer+div for autograd [bd-1tax]

tensor_corrcoef delegated to tensor_cov (now autograd-aware after the
previous commit) but then extracted the cov values, computed the
elementwise normalization in plain f64, and rebuilt a fresh
requires_grad=false leaf — discarding cov's autograd graph in the
process. PyTorch's torch.corrcoef is differentiable.

Composition now stays in the autograd tape end-to-end:

    cov         = tensor_cov(input)              // [N, N], autograd-aware
    diag        = tensor_diagonal(cov, 0)         // [N]
    diag_sqrt   = tensor_sqrt(diag)               // [N]
    denom       = tensor_outer(diag_sqrt, diag_sqrt)  // [N, N]: σ_i·σ_j
    corr_unsafe = tensor_div(cov, denom)          // NaN/inf at zero-σ
    corr        = where(denom == 0, 0, corr_unsafe)

The corrcoef formula corr[i,j] = cov[i,j] / sqrt(cov[i,i]·cov[j,j])
is rewritten as cov[i,j] / (sqrt(diag(cov))[i] · sqrt(diag(cov))[j])
so the broadcast denominator can be built via a single outer
product. The where-mask preserves PyTorch's "zero-variance variable
correlates as 0" convention without producing NaNs in the output.

Test: corrcoef_propagates_gradients_through_input asserts on a 2x3
input that backward produces finite gradients AND the row-sum-zero
shift-invariance property (corrcoef is shift-invariant along the
observation dim, just like cov). Both regressions — severed tape
and broken composition algebra — would fail this test.

All existing corrcoef tests (corrcoef_perfect_correlation,
corrcoef_negative_correlation) continue to pass — bit-for-bit
forward parity preserved.

Files closure for the corrcoef slice of frankentorch-1tax.

Workspace: 1127 ft-api tests green (1126 + 1 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
